name: calculator
description: Evaluates mathematical expressions using mathjs library

name: lua-executor
description: Executes raw Lua scripts in the Civilization V game context through the Bridge Service

Script.description: Raw Lua script to execute in the game context (e.g., 'return Game.GetActivePlayer()', 'print("Hello from Lua")')

Description.description: Description of what this script does

name: get-game-settings
description: Retrieves static game metadata including game speed, map type/size, difficulty, start era, max turns, and enabled victory types

PlayerID.description: Optional player ID to get player-specific information

name: get-metadata
description: Get a metadata value by key from the knowledge store

name: set-metadata
description: Set a metadata key-value pair

name: search-database
description: Searches across all database tools (technologies, policies, buildings, civilizations, units, flavors) using fuzzy matching and returns reranked results

Keywords.description: Keywords to search for across all database tools

MaxResults.description: Maximum number of results to return (default: 10)

name: get-technology
description: Retrieves technology information from the Civilization V game database with listing and fuzzy search capabilities

name: get-policy
description: Retrieves policy information from the Civilization V game database with listing and fuzzy search capabilities

name: get-building
description: Retrieves building information from the Civilization V game database with listing and fuzzy search capabilities

name: get-civilization
description: Retrieves civilization and leader information from the Civilization V game database with listing and fuzzy search capabilities

name: get-unit
description: Retrieves unit information from the Civilization V game database with listing and fuzzy search capabilities

name: get-economic-strategies
description: Retrieves AI economic strategy information including production (city) and overall (player) flavors

name: get-military-strategies
description: Retrieves AI military strategy information including production (city) and overall (player) flavors

name: get-victory-progress
description: Retrieves progress towards all victory types for all players, filtered by diplomatic visibility

PlayerID.description: Optional player ID to filter victory progress based on diplomatic visibility

name: get-military-report
description: Retrieves military report for a player including units organized by AI type and tactical zones with unit assignments

PlayerID.description: Player ID whose military report to retrieve

name: get-events
description: Retrieves a detailed list of recent game events

After.description: Only filter events after the ID

Before.description: Only filter events before or at the ID

PlayerID.description: Player ID visibility filter

Original.description: Send out original events before consolidation

name: get-diplomatic-events
description: Retrieves diplomatic events (wars, peace, deals, city-state relations, espionage, world congress) grouped by turn

PlayerID.description: Player ID whose perspective to view from (visibility filter)

OtherPlayerID.description: Only show events relevant to this other player

FromTurn.description: Only show events from this turn onwards

ToTurn.description: Only show events up to and including this turn

Formatted.description: Return formatted markdown summaries instead of raw event data

name: get-players
description: Retrieves a list of in-game players and their summary information

PlayerID.description: Optional player ID to filter for a specific player

name: get-opinions
description: Retrieves diplomatic opinions TO and FROM a player with all other alive major civilizations along with their static player information

PlayerID.description: Player ID whose opinions to retrieve

RevealAll.description: Optional flag to reveal all players regardless of met status (default: false)

name: get-cities
description: Retrieves city information from a player's perspective with optional owner filtering

PlayerID.description: Player ID from whose perspective to retrieve cities

Owner.description: Optional filter to retrieve only cities owned by this leader/civilization name

name: summarize-units
description: Gets an overview of all visible units for a player, grouped by civilization and AI type

PlayerID.description: The player ID to get unit visibility for

name: get-options
description: Retrieves available strategic options (technologies, policies, strategies) for a specific player

PlayerID.description: Player ID to retrieve strategic options for

Mode.description: Mode for retrieving options - 'Flavor' for tactical AI preferences, 'Strategy' for high-level strategies

name: set-strategy
description: Set a player's in-game strategy by names. Inputs must match exact values in the provided options.

PlayerID.description: ID of the player

GrandStrategy.description: The grand strategy name to set (and override)

EconomicStrategies.description: The economic strategy names to set (and override)

MilitaryStrategies.description: The military strategy names to set (and override)

Rationale.description: Briefly explain your rationale behind choosing this strategy set

Turn.description: Source turn for this action (-1 = use server's current turn)

name: set-persona
description: Set a player's diplomatic personality (1-10). These values control the in-game AI's diplomatic patterns and decision-making. Only send in values you intend to change.

VictoryCompetitiveness.description: How aggressively the AI reacts to others pursuing victories (1-10)

WonderCompetitiveness.description: How aggressively the AI reacts to others competing for wonders (1-10)

MinorCivCompetitiveness.description: How aggressively the AI reacts to others competing for city-state influence (1-10)

Boldness.description: Military risk-taking, territorial claim, and conquest desire (1-10)

WarBias.description: Likelihood to plan for or declare offensive war (1-10)

HostileBias.description: Tendency toward hostile relationships without direct wars (1-10)

WarmongerHate.description: How negatively AI reacts to warlike behaviors (1-10)

GuardedBias.description: Tendency to be guarded or cautiously defensive in diplomacy (1-10)

AfraidBias.description: Tendency to be afraid of stronger civs (1-10)

DiplomaticBalance.description: Increases relationship with non-competitive civilizations and peaceful resolution of wars (1-10)

Friendliness.description: Desire for friendship declarations and increases maximum DoFs (1-10)

WorkWithWillingness.description: Tendency to support or collaborate with allies. Increase opinions to shared friends (1-10)

WorkAgainstWillingness.description: Tendency to bond over shared enemies and jointly act against them (1-10)

Loyalty.description: Loyalty to allies. Lower values allow for backstabbing (1-10)

MinorCivFriendlyBias.description: Tendency to be friendly with city-states (1-10)

MinorCivNeutralBias.description: Tendency to be neutral with city-states (1-10)

MinorCivHostileBias.description: Tendency to be hostile with city-states (1-10)

Forgiveness.description: How quickly to forgive past transgressions (1-10)

Meanness.description: Aggressiveness in general. Demanding/bullying more while less likely to accept peace (1-10)

Chattiness.description: How often they initiate diplomatic contact (1-10)

PlayerID.description: ID of the player

Rationale.description: Briefly explain your rationale for adjusting these persona values

Turn.description: Source turn for this action (-1 = use server's current turn)

name: set-relationship
description: Set additive diplomatic modifiers between you and another MAJOR (non-city-state) civilization. Public or private modifiers add up in calculation.

PlayerID.description: ID of the player setting the relationship

TargetID.description: ID of the target MAJOR civilization

Rationale.description: Briefly explain your rationale for this relationship change

Turn.description: Source turn for this action (-1 = use server's current turn)

name: set-flavors
description: Set flavor values (0-100, 50 is balanced) and/or grand strategy to shape tactical AI preferences without changing ongoing queues. Only send in values you intend to change.

PlayerID.description: ID of the player

GrandStrategy.description: The grand strategy name to set (and override)

Flavors.description: Flavor values to set: 0 = forbid, 30 = enough, 50 = balanced, 70 = prioritize, 100 = emergency focus.

Rationale.description: Briefly explain your rationale for these adjustments

Turn.description: Source turn for this action (-1 = use server's current turn)

name: get-flavors
description: Retrieves flavor descriptions for AI preference tuning

name: unset-flavors
description: Clear all custom flavor values for a player, reverting to default AI preferences.

PlayerID.description: ID of the player

Turn.description: Source turn for this action (-1 = use server's current turn)

name: set-research
description: Set a player's next research technology by name. The in-game AI will start researching it after completing the current one.

PlayerID.description: ID of the player

Technology.description: Technology name to research next (None to clear)

Rationale.description: Briefly explain your rationale for selecting this technology

Turn.description: Source turn for this action (-1 = use server's current turn)

name: set-policy
description: Set a player's next policy selection by name. The in-game AI will be forced to select this policy or policy branch when making its next policy choice.

PlayerID.description: ID of the player

Policy.description: Policy name or branch name to adopt next (None to clear)

Rationale.description: Briefly explain your rationale for selecting this policy

Turn.description: Source turn for this action (-1 = use server's current turn)

name: keep-status-quo
description: Maintain the current in-game strategy and document the rationale

PlayerID.description: ID of the player

Mode.description: 'Flavor' to reapply custom flavors, 'Strategy' to reapply strategies (default)

Rationale.description: Briefly explain why the current strategic direction should be maintained.

Turn.description: Source turn for this action (-1 = use server's current turn)

name: relay-message
description: Relay a message from a diplomat to the leader, stored as a game event. Supports diplomatic messages and intelligence reports.

PlayerID.description: The receiving player ID (the leader being informed)

FromPlayerID.description: The player ID of the civilization the message concerns

Message.description: Message type: "diplomatic" for official communications, "intelligence" for gathered information

Confidence.description: Reliability assessment (0 = unreliable rumor, 9 = authoritative leader statement)

Importance.description: Strategic urgency (0 = background information, 9 = leader must reconsider strategy immediately; 7+ is considered important)

Memo.description: The analyst's memo: assessment, reaction, and contextual notes

name: present-decision
description: Present a turn's strategic options to the in-game human-control panel (fetches get-options and fires VoxDeorumHumanDecision into the game).

PlayerID.description: ID of the human strategist's player

Turn.description: Turn the decision is for (-1 = use server's current turn)

name: pause-game
description: Pause the game during the turn of a specific player

PlayerID.description: ID of the player whose turn should trigger a pause

name: resume-game
description: Resume the game during the turn of a specific player

PlayerID.description: ID of the player to resume the game for

name: set-production-mode
description: Enable or disable production mode (AI turn cooldown) in the DLL
